title: Red Hat Security Advisory: Red Hat build of MicroShift 4.17.49 security update
overview:
Red Hat build of MicroShift release 4.17.49 is now available with updates
to packages and images that include a security update.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact
of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which
gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from
the CVE link(s) in the References section.

full_text:
Red Hat Security Advisory: Red Hat build of MicroShift 4.17.49 security update

Red Hat build of MicroShift release 4.17.49 is now available with updates
to packages and images that include a security update.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact
of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which
gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from
the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Red Hat build of MicroShift release 4.17.49 is now available with updates
to packages and images that include a security update.

Red Hat Product Security has rated this update as having a security impact
of Moderate. A Common Vulnerability Scoring System (CVSS) base score, which
gives a detailed severity rating, is available for each vulnerability from
the CVE link(s) in the References section.

Updated Packages:
microshift-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.src.rpm
microshift-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.x86_64.rpm

microshift-release-info-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.noarch.rpm
microshift-selinux-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.noarch.rpm
microshift-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.aarch64.rpm
microshift-multus-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.aarch64.rpm
microshift-networking-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.aarch64.rpm
microshift-olm-4.17.49-202602161401.p0.g708a69a.assembly.4.17.49.el9.aarch64.rpm

CVE-2025-65637: A denial-of-service vulnerability in github.com/sirupsen/logrus occurs when Entry.Writer() processes a single-line payload larger than 64KB with no newline characters. Due to a limitation in Go’s internal bufio.Scanner, the read operation fails with a “token too long” error, causing the underlying writer pipe to close. In affected versions, this leaves the Writer interface unusable and can disrupt logging functionality, potentially degrading application availability.
